skill.name: web-build-deploy
skill.description: Build and deploy React applications with Vercel, Netlify, Docker, GitHub Actions, environment configuration, and deployment checks.
skill.summary: Guide React web app builds, hosting setup, CI/CD deployment, Docker packaging, and production readiness.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but the reviewed file is a Markdown deployment guide rather than executable skill code. The findings are mostly documentation examples for CLIs, dynamic imports, URLs, and environment variables; the remaining concern is operational risk because users may run commands that deploy services or handle secrets.

medium_findings.title: Deployment Commands Can Modify External Services
medium_findings.description: The guide includes commands for Vercel, Netlify, Docker, and GitHub Actions deployment workflows. These are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they can publish applications or change hosted services if a user runs them with real credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly contain deployment and container commands. The risk is operational impact, not malicious intent, because the commands match the stated purpose of the skill.

medium_findings.title: Environment and Secret Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: The guide shows environment variable setup and secret references for hosting, CI/CD, databases, and Sentry. These examples are normal deployment documentation, but users must avoid exposing private values through public prefixes or committed files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly discusses environment variables and secrets. The examples use placeholders and standard CI secret syntax, so the risk depends on how users adapt the guidance.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside Markdown code blocks that demonstrate build and deployment commands. No executable helper script, hidden runner, or automatic command invocation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows the detections are examples in SKILL.md. No separate script file or instruction to hide execution was present.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are React Code-Splitting Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import detections are React lazy loading examples for route and component splitting. They import local application modules and do not load remote code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is labeled Code Splitting and uses static local module paths. This is a standard React optimization pattern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Placeholder Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL detections are preview URL formats, placeholder API endpoints, and localhost examples. I did not find evidence that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in configuration snippets and use example domains or localhost. No network request code or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations do not contain cryptographic operations. Line 3 is metadata text, and line 31 is a Vercel rewrite destination entry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hash function, cipher, or cryptographic API at the reported lines. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Deployment CLIs and Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends deployment CLIs and a third-party GitHub Action. Users should verify package and action trust before running production deployments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and action references are explicit and relevant to deployment. They are not malicious, but they increase supply-chain and operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Files and Secret References
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references .env files, database URLs, CI secrets, and runtime environment variables. These are necessary topics for deployment, but misuse can expose credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text clearly covers secret and environment variable handling. The examples use standard patterns, so the risk is misuse rather than confirmed unsafe code.

content.value_statement: React deployments often fail because build settings, environment variables, and hosting targets differ by platform. This skill provides practical patterns for Vercel, Netlify, Docker, GitHub Actions, and production checks.
actual_capabilities: Provides Vercel deployment commands, configuration examples, preview deployment guidance, and environment variable setup.
Provides Netlify deploy commands, netlify.toml structure, redirects, headers, and build environment examples.
Shows Dockerfile, nginx, Docker Compose, and container run patterns for React single-page applications.
Includes GitHub Actions workflows for build, test, production deployment, and pull request preview deployment.
Covers Vite and Next.js environment variable patterns, build analysis, code splitting, caching, and monitoring checks.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not execute deployments by itself.
It focuses on React, Vite, and Next.js style web applications.
It provides examples that must be adapted to each project and hosting account.
It does not audit live infrastructure, cloud permissions, or secret values.

use_cases.title: Prepare a React App for Hosting
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose Vercel or Netlify settings, configure build output, and set environment variables.

use_cases.title: Add CI/CD Deployment
use_cases.description: Use the skill to draft GitHub Actions workflows that build, test, and deploy a React application.

use_cases.title: Package a Static App with Docker
use_cases.description: Use the skill to create a multi-stage Docker build with nginx routing for a single-page application.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Basic Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me deploy my React app. Ask which platform I use, then give me the build command, output directory, and environment variable steps.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Hosting Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my React hosting setup for Vercel or Netlify. Identify likely causes for routing, build output, or environment variable failures.

prompt_templates.title: Create a CI/CD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GitHub Actions workflow for my React app that installs dependencies, runs tests, builds, and deploys only from the main branch.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production deployment plan for my React app covering Docker, security headers, secrets, preview deployments, monitoring, and rollback checks.

output_examples.input: I need to deploy a Vite React app to Netlify.
output_examples.output: A concise Netlify setup with build command, publish directory, redirect handling for single-page routing, and environment variable placement.

output_examples.input: My app works locally but refresh routes return 404 in production.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis that points to missing single-page app fallback routing, with platform-specific configuration guidance.

output_examples.input: I want GitHub Actions to deploy only after tests pass.
output_examples.output: A workflow plan that separates dependency install, testing, building, secret usage, and production deploy gating.

best_practices: Review each generated command before running it against a production account.
Keep private values in hosting or CI secret stores, not committed environment files.
Test builds locally and in preview environments before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not commit real database URLs, tokens, or private keys into repository files.
Do not expose server-only secrets through public client-side environment variable prefixes.
Do not use third-party deployment actions without reviewing their source, version, and permissions.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy my app automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and configuration examples that a user or agent may choose to run.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Vercel, Netlify, Docker with nginx, and GitHub Actions deployment workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with environment variables?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains common Vite, Next.js, hosting, and CI environment variable patterns.

faq.question: Is it only for React apps?
faq.answer: It is primarily written for React web applications, especially Vite and Next.js projects.

faq.question: Does it include production readiness checks?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes security headers, monitoring, health checks, performance optimization, and deployment checklist items.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should verify commands, secrets, hosting permissions, build output, preview behavior, and third-party action trust.
